meaning_kannada: ನಿರೀಕ್ಷಿತ ಮಟ್ಟದಲ್ಲಿ ಯಶಸ್ವಿಯಾಗದ ವಿಷಯ (Nireekshita mattadalli yashasviyagada vishaya)
meaning_english: A situation or event which is much less impressive than expected.
To deal with a person who is unexpectedly troublesome or powerful.
A cause of dispute or quarrel.
To fail spectacularly or end suddenly and completely.

meaning_kannada: ಸುಳ್ಳು ಶೋಧನೆ ಅಥವಾ ಗೊಂದಲದ ಸ್ಥಿತಿ (Sullu shodhane athava gondalada sthiti)
meaning_english: A false invention or a complex and confusing situation.
To experience difficulties or problems.
To receive an insult without showing resentment.
Anything that is very common and easy to get.
To spoil someone's chances or plans.

meaning_kannada: ಜೀರ್ಣಿಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳುವ ಶಕ್ತಿ ಇರುವ ವ್ಯಕ್ತಿ (Jeernisikolluva shakti iruva vyakti)
meaning_english: Someone who can eat anything without getting sick.

meaning_kannada: ಹಳೆಯ ಘಟನೆಗಳ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಅಸಮಾಧಾನ (Haleya ghatanehala bagge asamadana)
meaning_english: Being upset for something that happened in the past.

word: Don't look a gift horse in the mouth
meaning_kannada: ಉಚಿತವಾಗಿ ಬಂದದ್ದರಲ್ಲಿ ತಪ್ಪು ಹುಡುಕಬೇಡಿ (Uchitavagi bandaddaralli tappu hudukabedi)
meaning_english: Don't find fault with something that has been received as a gift.

word: Between the cup and the lips
meaning_english: On the point of achievement but not yet certain.
A result that is still unclear and can go either way.

word: To kill two birds with one stone
meaning_kannada: ಒಂದೇ ಏಟಿಗೆ ಎರಡು ಹಕ್ಕಿ (Onde etige eradu hakki)
meaning_english: To achieve two aims at once with a single action.
In a precarious or risky position.

word: By the skin of one's teeth
meaning_english: By a very narrow margin.

meaning_kannada: ಸೋಲಿಗೆ ಕಾರಣವಾಗುವ ಕೊನೆಯ ಘಟನೆ (Solige karanavaguva koneya ghatane)
meaning_english: The final act or event that brings about total failure.
Someone who has similar traits to their parents.
The main source of income or livelihood.
In a carelessly hurried or disorderly manner.
A very long time.
A situation that is completely different.
Used to say that something is completed easily or all set.
To cheer sarcastically or show disapproval.
